nouns.summary: The text says that the speaker will divide “many” to the subject ("him"). This division is presented as a reward given because of what the subject did with his life and guilt-related actions.
The speaker states that “mighty ones” will be divided as spoil. Their distribution is connected to the subject’s willingness to face death and his role regarding sin and transgressors.
“Spoil” is described as something that will be divided to him. It functions as the outcome of the subject’s self-giving to death and his bearing of sin.
The subject is said to have poured out his soul unto death. The noun is tied directly to the subject’s active, sacrificial commitment leading to death.
Death is the endpoint of the subject’s pouring out of his soul. The text portrays his participation in death as the cause for the subsequent division of spoil and the final outcome regarding sinners.

The subject is said to be “numbered with transgressors,” meaning he is counted among them. Later, the subject’s actions are also directed toward transgressors, stating that he will make intercession for them.
The text states that the subject bore “sin,” specifically the sin of many. This noun is connected to the subject’s action of taking responsibility for sin.
The subject is described as actively making intercession for the transgressors. This ties the noun to an ongoing, active role of advocacy on behalf of those who have transgressed.
